criteria: `skills/` and `hooks/` directories exist
At least one platform manifest directory present
`.gitignore` exists
`README.md` exists
`LICENSE` exists
Bootstrap skill (`using-*`) exists with `SKILL.md`

Skill-agent responsibility boundary: skills handle orchestration (scope detection, dispatch, result composition), agents handle execution (checks, scoring, reporting). No duplication of execution details — agent file is the single source of truth
Skill directory names match `name` field in SKILL.md frontmatter
Agent files in `agents/` are self-contained — body includes complete execution protocol (≥5 non-empty body lines), not just a pointer
Writable agents (those without `disallowedTools: Edit` or `disallowedTools: Write`) have `isolation: "worktree"` set to prevent main working tree conflicts
Skill inline fallback blocks (handling "subagent unavailable") reference the corresponding agent file (`agents/*.md`) rather than re-implementing the execution logic
Each target platform has its manifest file present
Manifest JSON is valid (parseable, no syntax errors)
Cursor manifest paths (`skills`, `hooks`) resolve to existing directories/files
Manifest metadata (name, version, description) is filled in
Author and repository fields are populated
OpenCode `.js` plugin files contain `module.exports` or `export` statement
`claude plugin validate` (or `/plugin validate`) passes without errors — quick schema check for `plugin.json`, frontmatter, and `hooks.json` (Claude Code environments only)
`.version-bump.json` exists and is valid
All files listed in `.version-bump.json` actually exist
All listed files have the same version string (no drift)
Every platform manifest is listed in `.version-bump.json`
`bundles-forge bump-version --check` exits 0
`bundles-forge bump-version --audit` finds no undeclared version strings
YAML frontmatter present (between `---` delimiters)
`name` field exists in frontmatter
`description` field exists in frontmatter
`name` uses only letters, numbers, hyphens
`description` starts with "Use when..."
`description` describes triggering conditions, not workflow summary
`description` is under 250 characters (truncated in skill listing beyond this)
Frontmatter total under 1024 characters
SKILL.md body under 500 lines
Skill has Overview section
Skill has Common Mistakes section
Heavy reference content (100+ lines) extracted to supporting files
Token budget: bootstrap skill body ≤ 200 lines (warning); regular skill reports estimated token count when high (info)
`allowed-tools` frontmatter references scripts/paths that actually exist
Conditional blocks (`If ... unavailable` etc.) over 30 lines should be in `references/`
All `<project>:<skill-name>` references resolve to existing skills
No broken relative-path references to supporting files
Text references to subdirectories (`references/`, `templates/`, etc.) match actual skill directory contents
No orphan files in `references/` — every `.md` and `.json` file is referenced by `SKILL.md` or a sibling reference file
`hooks/` directory exists
`hooks/session-start` (Bash) exists
`hooks/run-hook.cmd` (polyglot wrapper) exists
`session-start` references SKILL.md or emits skill list
`session-start` handles all target platforms (three-way: CURSOR_PLUGIN_ROOT, CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT, COPILOT_CLI/fallback)
JSON escaping is correct (backslashes, quotes, newlines, tabs)
Claude Code hook config uses `run-hook.cmd session-start`; Cursor uses `./hooks/session-start` directly
`hooks.json` includes top-level `description` field and per-handler `timeout`
OpenClaw hook-pack exists (`hooks/*/HOOK.md` + `handler.js`) with valid YAML frontmatter and `events` declaration
OpenClaw hook-pack `handler.js` uses ESM `export default` and filters events early
`session-start` exits 0 on failure (no-op, does not block session)
`tests/` directory exists
At least one test per target platform
Tests verify skill discovery (skills appear in available list)
Tests verify bootstrap injection (`session-start` output / content loads)
Each skill has a test prompts file (`tests/prompts/<skill-name>.yml` or `skills/<name>/tests/prompts.yml`)
Test prompts include both should-trigger and should-not-trigger samples
Test prompts cover all major branch paths of the skill
Most recent A/B eval result exists in `.bundles-forge/evals/`
Most recent chain eval result exists in `.bundles-forge/evals/`
Skill names in AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / README match skills/ directory
Cross-references (`project:skill`) in all .md files resolve to existing skills
CLAUDE.md Platform Manifests table matches `.version-bump.json`
Skill scripts referenced in CLAUDE.md exist at their declared `skills/.../scripts/` paths
Agent names in CLAUDE.md match agents/ directory
README.md and README.zh.md hard data (skills, agents, commands, links) in sync
docs/*.md and docs/*.zh.md pairs have consistent hard data (tables, commands, links)
Each docs/*.md guide has a `> **Canonical source:**` declaration pointing to an existing skill or agent file
Key numbers in docs/*.md guides match their canonical source (e.g., attack surface count, category count, target count)
No undeclared circular dependencies in the workflow graph. Cycles declared via `<!-- cycle:a,b -->` in `## Integration` are demoted to Info
All skills are reachable from entry points (skills referenced by `using-*` bootstrap). Skills declaring "Called by: user directly" in `## Integration` are exempt
Terminal skills (no outgoing cross-references) have an `## Outputs` section documenting final deliverables and are clearly identifiable as workflow endpoints
Skills referenced by other skills have an `## Inputs` section declaring expected artifacts
For each edge A→B, at least one artifact ID in A's `## Outputs` matches an ID in B's `## Inputs`
Skills with workflow dependencies document them in an `## Integration` section with `**Calls:**` and/or `**Called by:**` blocks
Workflow chain has no semantically unreasonable circular dependencies — declared cycles (`<!-- cycle:a,b -->`) have a clear rationale (e.g. feedback loop between audit and optimize)
`## Inputs` and `## Outputs` sections contain meaningful semantic descriptions — not empty, not placeholder text (e.g. "TBD", "TODO"), each artifact has a name and one-line purpose

For newly added skills: Integration section uses the correct project cross-reference prefix, `**Calls:**` / `**Called by:**` declarations are symmetric with the skills they reference (if A calls B, B should list A in Called by)
Chain A/B Eval: for each workflow chain involving focus skills, an end-to-end scenario produces no broken handoffs (evaluator reports all transitions as "smooth" or "acceptable")
Newly added skills can be triggered and exit correctly within their workflow chain context — the skill activates on expected input and produces output consumable by the next skill in the chain
Instructions to read `.env`, `.ssh/`, `credentials`, `secrets`, `tokens`, `api_key` files
Instructions to include file contents in responses or outputs sent externally
References to reading `~/.config/`, `~/.aws/`, `~/.kube/` or similar config directories
Instructions to access `process.env`, `$ENV`, or environment variables beyond documented needs
Instructions containing `rm -rf`, `rmdir /s`, `del /f` on broad paths
Instructions for `git push --force`, `git reset --hard` on main/master
Instructions containing `DROP TABLE`, `DELETE FROM` without WHERE clause
Instructions to modify or overwrite files outside the project directory
Phrases like "ignore previous instructions", "override safety", "disable verification"
Instructions to skip git hooks (`--no-verify`), bypass linters, or disable tests
Instructions that conflict with `using-<project>` instruction priority (claiming higher priority than user instructions)
Use of `<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>` or similar emphasis tags outside the bootstrap skill
Unicode homoglyphs, zero-width characters, right-to-left override characters
Base64-encoded content that decodes to instructions or commands
Excessively long line (>500 chars) — may indicate obfuscated or machine-generated content
`curl`, `wget`, `nc`, `ncat`, `telnet`, `ssh` calls to external hosts
Any URL or IP address that is not localhost/127.0.0.1
Piping environment variables or file contents to network commands
DNS lookups (`dig`, `nslookup`, `host`) that could encode data in queries
Reading `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`, or similar secrets
Reading environment variables beyond `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT`, `CURSOR_PLUGIN_ROOT`
Shell hook scripts not using `set -euo pipefail` (missing error handling); not reported for Python-only hooks
Writing to `.bashrc`, `.zshrc`, `.profile`, `.bash_profile`, or shell config files
Creating cron jobs, systemd services, or launchd agents
Modifying PATH, installing global packages (`npm -g`, `pip install --user`)
Creating files outside the project directory or `~/.config/<project>/`
Running `chmod` with setuid/setgid bits
`type: "http"` in hooks.json — HTTP hooks send tool input/output to external URLs
External URLs in hook config (not localhost/127.0.0.1)
Writing to `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` — injects env vars (including PATH) into all subsequent Bash commands
`eval()`, `new Function()`, `vm.runInNewContext()`
`child_process.exec()`, `child_process.spawn()`, `execSync()`
`require('child_process')` or dynamic `import()` of system modules
Dynamic `require()` or `import()` with variable paths
`fetch()`, `http.request()`, `https.request()`, `net.connect()` to external hosts
WebSocket connections to external servers
Any network-related imports (`http`, `https`, `net`, `dgram`, `dns`)
`process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` or similar secret access
Broad `process.env` access beyond documented needs
Reading files outside the plugin's own directory tree
Message transforms that inject content not derived from the project's own SKILL.md files
Message transforms that modify existing user messages (beyond prepending bootstrap)
Missing guard against double-injection (checking for `EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT` before injecting)
Instructions to "ignore", "override", or "bypass" safety guidelines or user instructions
Instructions to access credentials, secrets, or API keys
Instructions to make network requests or exfiltrate data
Instructions that expand scope beyond the agent's stated role
Instructions that claim elevated permissions ("you have full system access")
Missing scope constraints (agent prompt doesn't limit what files/actions are in scope)
Agent prompt instructs dispatching other agents/subagents — subagents cannot nest; orchestration must stay in the parent skill
Network calls (`curl`, `wget`, `fetch`) in scripts not documented as needing network
System modification patterns (same as HK8-HK12)
Reading or transmitting sensitive files or environment variables
Scripts that accept unsanitized user input passed to `eval` or command execution
Scripts that download and execute remote code
Scripts missing `set -euo pipefail` or equivalent error handling
Hardcoded credential in MCP server config (Authorization, api_key, token, secret, password)
`headersHelper` field executes arbitrary shell commands
Env var value embedded directly instead of using `${VAR}` expansion
MCP server URL uses plain HTTP instead of HTTPS
Absolute path in command field (may not be portable)
`../` path references in `plugin.json` component paths, hook commands, or MCP/LSP configs — after marketplace install, the plugin is cached and `../` paths break
Absolute paths in plugin configs — installed plugins should use `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` or relative `./` paths
Symlinks to external directories — legitimate but should be documented in README
`userConfig` field contains key names suggesting secrets (token, key, secret, password, credential, auth) but `sensitive` is not set to `true`
`userConfig` sensitive values referenced via `${user_config.KEY}` in skill or agent content — sensitive values should only appear in MCP/LSP/hook configs, not in conversation-visible content
Hook scripts or MCP configs write to `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` — data written here is lost on plugin update; should use `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` instead
No dependency caching pattern detected when plugin bundles MCP servers with npm dependencies — consider adding a SessionStart hook for `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` install
